L1.area: Cryptographic data serialization and deserialization layer

L2.description: A null pointer dereference vulnerability exists in the PKCS#7 certificate deserialization logic. When a PKCS#7 structure contains no certificates, the code attempts to access a member of a null pointer without first verifying it is non-null, leading to a segfault and denial of service.

L3.area: PKCS#7 signed data certificate loading in the OpenSSL backend
L3.description: A null pointer dereference vulnerability exists when loading PKCS#7 bundles that contain no certificates. The deserialization code accesses the signed data structure's certificate field without first checking if the signed data pointer is null, causing a segfault and denial of service when processing a bundle with an empty certificate set.

ground_truth.reason: The PKCS#7 certificate loading code dereferences the signed data pointer without checking for null first. When a PKCS#7 bundle contains no certificates, this pointer is null, causing a segfault.
